papers.title: Energy-differential measurement of the $^{\mathrm{nat}}\mathrm{C}(n,p)$ and $^{\mathrm{nat}}\mathrm{C}(n,d)$ reactions at the n_TOF facility at CERN
papers.summary: Author(s): P. Žugec et al. (The n_TOF Collaboration)Energy-differential cross section of the $^{\mathrm{nat}}\mathrm{C}(n,p)$ and $^{\mathrm{nat}}\mathrm{C}(n,d)$ reactions was measured at the neutron time-of-flight facility n_TOF at CERN. papers.title: Isotopic evolution of single-particle strengths and energies in the 50–82 shell
papers.authors: A. Kundu, S. Santra, S. Kailas, A. Pal, D. Chattopadhyay, and R. Tripathi
papers.summary: Author(s): A. Kundu, S. Santra, S. Kailas, A. Pal, D. Chattopadhyay, and R. TripathiOdd-neutron and odd-proton spectroscopic strengths have been obtained for low-lying bound states in the $N,Z$ = 50–82 major shell, populated through $1n$- and $1p$-transfer reactions on the even-$A$ $^{112−124}\mathrm{Sn}$ isotopes. While most proton orbitals in the 50–82 shell exhibit predominantly…[Phys. Rev. C 113, 044622] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026

papers.title: Computational schemes for the Magnus expansion of the in-medium similarity renormalization group
papers.summary: Author(s): Matthias HeinzThe in-medium similarity renormalization group (IMSRG) is a popular many-body method used for computations of nuclei. It solves the many-body Schrödinger equation through a continuous unitary transformation of the many-body Hamiltonian. The IMSRG transformation is typically truncated at the normal-o…[Phys. Rev. C 113, L041301] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
